description: Right-side crossing truck on a normal road at very short TTC, with the nominal left escape corridor blocked and a pedestrian on that same left side, plus a close follower behind the ego. This stress-tests the low-TTC boundary of the existing right-crossing/left-blocked/pedestrian/follower rule, which currently starts at TTC 1.4 s.
hypothesis: Below the existing 1.4 s TTC boundary, the dominant maneuver should switch from evasive left-drift to hard braking/hold-lane unless rear gap is critically small, in which case minimal rightward bias behind the crossing truck may dominate.
